turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="bg" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#fff7d1" />
      <stop offset="40%" stop-color="#ffe08a" />
      <stop offset="70%" stop-color="#7aa0e0" />
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#0b1b2b" />
    </linearGradient>
    <radialGradient id="doorLight" cx="50%" cy="70%" r="60%">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#fff8cf" stop-opacity="1" />
      <stop offset="45%" stop-color="#ffe089" stop-opacity="0.95" />
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#ffdb6b" stop-opacity="0.6" />
    </radialGradient>
    <linearGradient id="pathGrad" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#fff7d1" stop-opacity="0.95" />
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#cda85a" stop-opacity="0.9" />
    </linearGradient>
  </defs>

  <!-- background -->
  <rect width="512" height="512" fill="url(#bg)" />

  <!-- light rays from doorway -->
  <g opacity="0.95">
    <polygon points="256,240 236,90 276,90" fill="#fff6c3" opacity="0.25" />
    <polygon points="256,240 206,70 226,70" fill="#fff3a8" opacity="0.18" />
    <polygon points="256,240 306,70 286,70" fill="#fff3a8" opacity="0.18" />
    <polygon points="256,240 166,130 186,120" fill="#fff7c9" opacity="0.12" />
    <polygon points="256,240 346,130 326,120" fill="#fff7c9" opacity="0.12" />
  </g>

  <!-- path leading out (perspective trapezoid) -->
  <polygon points="166,420 346,420 460,512 52,512" fill="url(#pathGrad)" opacity="0.95" />

  <!-- doorway frame (outer) -->
  <path d="M146 420 L146 380 A110 110 0 0 1 366 380 L366 420 Z" fill="#2b2b2b" />
  <!-- inner glowing arch -->
  <path d="M166 420 L166 390 A90 90 0 0 1 346 390 L346 420 Z" fill="url(#doorLight)" />

  <!-- small radiating beams inside arch for glow -->
  <g fill="#fff9d9" opacity="0.6">
    <rect x="250" y="310" width="12" height="90" transform="rotate(-8 256 355)" />
    <rect x="254" y="310" width="12" height="90" transform="rotate(8 260 355)" />
    <rect x="238" y="315" width="8" height="60" transform="rotate(-18 242 345)" />
    <rect x="276" y="315" width="8" height="60" transform="rotate(18 280 345)" />
  </g>

  <!-- person silhouette stepping out (simple shapes) -->
  <g transform="translate(0, -6)">
    <!-- legs -->
    <path d="M238 392 L248 392 L262 442 L246 442 Z" fill="#0b1016" />
    <path d="M264 392 L274 392 L294 442 L276 442 Z" fill="#0b1016" />
    <!-- body -->
    <path d="M246 338 C246 338 256 316 266 338 L282 338 C275 314 256 314 246 338 Z" fill="#0b1016" />
    <rect x="254" y="338" width="14" height="36" rx="6" fill="#0b1016" />
    <!-- arms raised -->
    <path d="M236 344 C216 320 200 314 198 320 C214 332 236 344 236 344 Z" fill="#0b1016" transform="translate(32,0) rotate(-14 256 348)" />
    <path d="M284 344 C304 320 320 314 322 320 C306 332 284 344 284 344 Z" fill="#0b1016" transform="translate(-28,0) rotate(14 256 348)" />
    <!-- head -->
    <circle cx="256" cy="322" r="14" fill="#0b1016" />
  </g>

  <!-- dove (simple stylized) -->
  <g transform="translate(40,-20)">
    <path d="M420 120 C402 108 384 112 368 120 C362 124 352 122 348 116 C340 104 320 98 304 106 C368 94 412 118 420 120 Z" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.98" />
    <path d="M382 118 C392 110 412 112 424 124 C416 120 398 124 382 118 Z" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.98" />
    <!-- olive branch -->
    <path d="M430 130 C436 126 444 126 450 132 C444 130 436 132 430 130 Z" fill="#6aa84f" />
    <path d="M442 136 C448 132 456 132 462 138 C456 136 448 138 442 136 Z" fill="#6aa84f" />
  </g>

  <!-- subtle vignette for focus -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="480" rx="220" ry="60" fill="#000" opacity="0.12" />

</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

I’ll depict a silhouette stepping from darkness into light, emphasizing hope and upliftment. The background will transition from dark blue at the bottom to light golden at the top to enhance this feeling. The arched doorway will have bright golden light shining out.**Creating a universal salvation symbol**\n\nI’m considering a visual for salvation, involving a white dove with an olive branch near the top to symbolize peace. I might include a cross, but since it’s a religious symbol, I want to keep it more universal. message.content:
{
  "svg": "<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="bg" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#fff7d1" />
      <stop offset="40%" stop-color="#ffe08a" />
      <stop offset="70%" stop-color="#7aa0e0" />
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#0b1b2b" />
    </linearGradient>
    <radialGradient id="doorLight" cx="50%" cy="70%" r="60%">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#fff8cf" stop-opacity="1" />
      <stop offset="45%" stop-color="#ffe089" stop-opacity="0.95" />
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#ffdb6b" stop-opacity="0.6" />
    </radialGradient>
    <linearGradient id="pathGrad" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#fff7d1" stop-opacity="0.95" />
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#cda85a" stop-opacity="0.9" />
    </linearGradient>
  </defs>

  <!-- background -->
  <rect width="512" height="512" fill="url(#bg)" />

  <!-- light rays from doorway -->
  <g opacity="0.95">
    <polygon points="256,240 236,90 276,90" fill="#fff6c3" opacity="0.25" />
    <polygon points="256,240 206,70 226,70" fill="#fff3a8" opacity="0.18" />
    <polygon points="256,240 306,70 286,70" fill="#fff3a8" opacity="0.18" />
    <polygon points="256,240 166,130 186,120" fill="#fff7c9" opacity="0.12" />
    <polygon points="256,240 346,130 326,120" fill="#fff7c9" opacity="0.12" />
  </g>

  <!-- path leading out (perspective trapezoid) -->
  <polygon points="166,420 346,420 460,512 52,512" fill="url(#pathGrad)" opacity="0.95" />

  <!-- doorway frame (outer) -->
  <path d="M146 420 L146 380 A110 110 0 0 1 366 380 L366 420 Z" fill="#2b2b2b" />
  <!-- inner glowing arch -->
  <path d="M166 420 L166 390 A90 90 0 0 1 346 390 L346 420 Z" fill="url(#doorLight)" />

  <!-- small radiating beams inside arch for glow -->
  <g fill="#fff9d9" opacity="0.6">
    <rect x="250" y="310" width="12" height="90" transform="rotate(-8 256 355)" />
    <rect x="254" y="310" width="12" height="90" transform="rotate(8 260 355)" />
    <rect x="238" y="315" width="8" height="60" transform="rotate(-18 242 345)" />
    <rect x="276" y="315" width="8" height="60" transform="rotate(18 280 345)" />
  </g>

  <!-- person silhouette stepping out (simple shapes) -->
  <g transform="translate(0, -6)">
    <!-- legs -->
    <path d="M238 392 L248 392 L262 442 L246 442 Z" fill="#0b1016" />
    <path d="M264 392 L274 392 L294 442 L276 442 Z" fill="#0b1016" />
    <!-- body -->
    <path d="M246 338 C246 338 256 316 266 338 L282 338 C275 314 256 314 246 338 Z" fill="#0b1016" />
    <rect x="254" y="338" width="14" height="36" rx="6" fill="#0b1016" />
    <!-- arms raised -->
    <path d="M236 344 C216 320 200 314 198 320 C214 332 236 344 236 344 Z" fill="#0b1016" transform="translate(32,0) rotate(-14 256 348)" />
    <path d="M284 344 C304 320 320 314 322 320 C306 332 284 344 284 344 Z" fill="#0b1016" transform="translate(-28,0) rotate(14 256 348)" />
    <!-- head -->
    <circle cx="256" cy="322" r="14" fill="#0b1016" />
  </g>

  <!-- dove (simple stylized) -->
  <g transform="translate(40,-20)">
    <path d="M420 120 C402 108 384 112 368 120 C362 124 352 122 348 116 C340 104 320 98 304 106 C368 94 412 118 420 120 Z" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.98" />
    <path d="M382 118 C392 110 412 112 424 124 C416 120 398 124 382 118 Z" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.98" />
    <!-- olive branch -->
    <path d="M430 130 C436 126 444 126 450 132 C444 130 436 132 430 130 Z" fill="#6aa84f" />
    <path d="M442 136 C448 132 456 132 462 138 C456 136 448 138 442 136 Z" fill="#6aa84f" />
  </g>

  <!-- subtle vignette for focus -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="480" rx="220" ry="60" fill="#000" opacity="0.12" />

</svg>"
}
message.reasoning:
**Designing a visual for "salvation"**

I need to create a visual representation of "salvation" without using any text. I’ll focus on symbols like a cross, light rays, open hands, a dove, a heart, a pathway, and an open door. The design must be simple, using vector shapes. The central cross will have glowing rays, an open door at the bottom leads to a path, and a dove will fly above. I want to avoid any religious bias and convey broader concepts of rescue instead. I’ll depict a silhouette stepping from darkness into light, emphasizing hope and upliftment. The background will transition from dark blue at the bottom to light golden at the top to enhance this feeling. The arched doorway will have bright golden light shining out.**Creating a universal salvation symbol**

I’m considering a visual for salvation, involving a white dove with an olive branch near the top to symbolize peace. I might include a cross, but since it’s a religious symbol, I want to keep it more universal. I’ll use SVG elements without scripts, incorporating linear gradients for the sky and radial gradients for light. Simple shapes will make up the design, with a dark navy background transitioning to gold light. The arched doorway will lead upwards, with a silhouette of a person near the door and light rays fanning out above.**Crafting the SVG for salvation**

I need to make sure the silhouette of the person is dark against the golden light in the arch, emphasizing their presence when stepping onto the path with arms raised. The dove with an olive leaf will appear near the upper right, and I’ll ensure there’s no text or numbers. I’m going to return the SVG in JSON format, properly escaping the quotes for the string. I’ll start by constructing the SVG with specified attributes and a linear gradient for the background, potentially adding a radial gradient for the door light.**Creating the arch for the doorway**

I need to craft an arch top for the doorway using a path that defines an outer and inner arch frame. The outer arch should be larger, while the inner one will provide the frame. messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

**Generating one-word guesses**

I need to come up with 20 one-word guesses that describe the drawing. It seems to be a stylized figure emerging from a tunnel, possibly celebrating as it jumps into bright light. Words like "freedom," "escape," "sunrise," and "victory" come to mind, among others. I want to order them by my confidence, leading with "escape" or "freedom" as the top choices. I’ll ensure no repeats and exactly 20 words, all in English.**Finalizing word list**

I had both "emerge" and "emergence" in mind but decided to go with "escape" first. Then I’ll add "freedom," "emerge," "exit," "breakthrough," "sunrise," "light," "victory," "celebrate," "liberation," "tunnel," "doorway," "triumph," "hope," "run," "jump," "silhouette," "release," "portal," and for the last one, I think "rebirth" fits better than "outcome." So here's the final ordered list of 20 words, ensuring no spaces or repeats.
